original_hypothesis.title: State-dependent hooks can be manipulated to DoS stopRent, locking lender assets
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: The stopRent function calls _removeHooks, which executes IHook(target).onStop(). If the hook reverts, the entire stopRent transaction reverts. A malicious renter can manipulate the external state that the hook depends on (e.g., a staking contract) to ensure the hook always reverts, permanently preventing the rental from being stopped and keeping the NFT in their Safe.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: Hooks introduce external dependencies into the critical path of asset recovery. Validating this requires analyzing the specific hook implementations approved by the protocol to see if their success depends on renter-controlled state.
deployment_assumptions: Protocol approves hooks that interact with external state (e.g., staking/unstaking) which can fail based on user actions
original_hypothesis.config_evidence: Guard.sol exposes updateHookStatus, allowing arbitrary external contracts to be added to the critical path

proof_of_signal.pattern: try IHook(target).onStop() catches errors and explicitly reverts the transaction

preconditions: An active rental uses a hook
The hook's onStop function can revert based on external state controlled by the renter

initial_economic_evidence.suspicious_calculation: Asset recovery is strictly dependent on external hook success
initial_economic_evidence.manipulation_vector: Renter alters external state (e.g., withdraws all liquidity from a pool the hook checks) so onStop reverts
initial_economic_evidence.profit_hypothesis: Renter retains indefinite use of the rented NFT without paying additional rent, effectively stealing its utility value

economic_questions: What specific hooks are currently approved?
Do any approved hooks have revert conditions tied to renter actions?
validation_requirements: Attempt to call stopRent and verify it fails

preliminary_attack_economics.potential_profit_range: Ongoing utility value of the NFT

market_conditions_analysis.market_impact_assessment: Loss of asset control for lenders

potential_victim_impact.estimated_impact_per_user: Permanent loss of access to their NFT
potential_victim_impact.total_value_at_risk: Value of all NFTs using the vulnerable hook

validation_result.impact_summary: If a hook's onStop() reverts for any reason (external state manipulation, bug, or admin disabling the hook), the stopRent transaction is permanently blocked, locking the lender's NFT in the renter's Safe.
validation_result.detailed_analysis: The _removeHooks function in Stop.sol uses a try/catch block that catches ALL revert types (Error, Panic, raw bytes) and immediately re-reverts them. Since hooks are committed in the rental order hash, they cannot be omitted when calling stopRent. This creates an unrecoverable DoS if any hook's onStop() reverts. There are multiple paths to trigger this: (1) A renter manipulates external state the hook depends on so onStop always reverts; (2) GUARD_ADMIN changes hookStatus to disable onStop bit after rental creation, causing the STORE.hookOnStop(target) check to fail; (3) The hook contract has a bug or unhandled edge case. In all cases, the rental is permanently locked because the caller must provide the original hooks (they're part of the order hash verified by STORE.removeRentals), and those hooks must succeed.
validation_result.code_evidence_assessment: The code evidence is clear and unambiguous. Stop.sol's _removeHooks catches all hook reverts and re-throws them. The hooks are part of the order hash (verified in Signer._deriveRentalOrderHash), so they cannot be omitted. There is no admin override or emergency bypass to skip hooks during stop.

vulnerable_code_snippets.vulnerability_explanation: The try/catch catches ALL exception types and immediately re-reverts. Any failure in the hook's onStop() function propagates as a revert in stopRent, with no fallback or bypass mechanism.
Hook removal is mandatory before any other stop operations. If _removeHooks reverts, no assets can be reclaimed and no storage can be updated.

Hooks are included in the order hash. The caller of stopRent must provide the exact same hooks from creation, as STORE.removeRentals verifies the hash. Hooks cannot be omitted to bypass the failing _removeHooks call.

calling_sequence: 1. User calls stopRent(order) with the original RentalOrder (including hooks)
5. _removeHooks checks STORE.hookOnStop(target) - if hook status was changed, reverts with Shared_DisabledHook
6. If hook status OK, calls IHook(target).onStop() in try/catch
7. If onStop reverts (any reason), catch block re-reverts the entire transaction
8. stopRent reverts - _reclaimRentedItems, settlePayment, removeRentals never execute
9. Rental remains active in storage, NFT stays locked in renter's Safe
execution_path_analysis.vulnerable_code_path: stopRent → _removeHooks → STORE.hookOnStop(target) [revert if disabled] or IHook(target).onStop() [revert caught and re-thrown]. Either path causes stopRent to revert entirely, blocking all downstream operations (reclaim, payment settlement, storage cleanup). The hooks cannot be omitted because _deriveRentalOrderHash includes them, and STORE.removeRentals verifies the hash matches.

deployment_assumptions_checked: Protocol approves hooks that interact with external state (e.g., staking/unstaking) which can fail based on user actions
config_validation.deployment_evidence: DeployProtocol.s.sol grants GUARD_ADMIN role to deployer but does not call updateHookPath or updateHookStatus. No hooks are configured at deployment time. Hooks are configured post-deployment by GUARD_ADMIN.

validation_result.attack_scenario_validation: Attack scenario 1 (state manipulation): Renter creates a rental with a hook that interacts with a staking contract. Before rental expiry, renter manipulates the staking contract state (e.g., withdraws staked tokens through the Safe via allowed transactions) so hook.onStop() reverts. Nobody can call stopRent successfully. Attack scenario 2 (admin action): GUARD_ADMIN disables the onStop bit for a hook after rental creation. stopRent calls STORE.hookOnStop(target) which returns false, causing revert with Shared_DisabledHook. Attack scenario 3 (hook bug): Hook contract has an unhandled edge case that causes onStop to revert. All three scenarios permanently lock the rental.
validation_result.impact_assessment: HIGH - Lender permanently loses access to their NFT, which remains locked in the renter's Safe. The renter effectively gets indefinite free use of the NFT. There is no emergency mechanism or admin override to recover the assets. The protocol has no way to force-stop a rental when hooks fail.
recommended_mitigations: Make hook execution in _removeHooks non-blocking: catch exceptions and emit a warning event instead of re-reverting, so stopRent can proceed even if hooks fail
Add an emergency admin function that can stop rentals while skipping hook execution
Store the hook status snapshot at rental creation time and use that snapshot during stop, rather than checking current hookStatus
Add a timeout mechanism: if a rental exceeds endTimestamp by a grace period, allow stopping without hook execution
validation_result.replication_plan: 1. Deploy protocol with all contracts. 2. Create a mock hook contract whose onStop() checks an external boolean flag and reverts if the flag is false. 3. As GUARD_ADMIN, register the hook via updateHookPath and updateHookStatus (bitmap=0x07). 4. Create a rental order that includes this hook. 5. Set the external boolean flag to false (simulating renter state manipulation). 6. Attempt to call stopRent - verify it reverts. 7. Also test: change hookStatus to disable onStop bit (bitmap=0x03), then attempt stopRent - verify it reverts with Shared_DisabledHook.
uncertainties: No hook implementations are provided in the codebase, so the specific external state dependencies are hypothetical
The actual hooks that GUARD_ADMIN would approve in production are unknown
The GUARD_ADMIN could potentially re-enable a disabled hook to unblock stopped rentals (but cannot fix a hook that reverts due to external state)
additional_testing_needed: Test with a mock hook that reverts in onStop() and verify stopRent is permanently blocked
Test the admin hook status change scenario (disable onStop bit after rental creation)
Test whether stopRentBatch is also affected (it is - same _removeHooks call)
Verify there is no alternative code path to stop a rental without hook execution
